title: Subqueries, CTEs, Set Operations, and Relational Reasoning · Relational Lens: Correlated Exists
summary: Operations needs reusable query stages for backlog comparisons, missing relationships, and cohorts across time windows. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql correlated exists increment in a composable backlog-analysis query pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish correlated subqueries and use EXISTS or NOT EXISTS for semijoin and antijoin questions.
Choose scalar, row, and table subqueries whose cardinality matches their surrounding expression.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Compare each ticket with its team's effort and severity peers, retain teams with no resolved work, and rank ties deliberately. Build named query stages, one changed window of 4 rows, and a reconciliation total. Competency probe: Distinguish correlated subqueries and use EXISTS or NOT EXISTS for semijoin and antijoin questions. A defensible solution applies Correlated Exists, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a composable backlog-analysis query pipeline.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-correlated-exists — Distinguish correlated subqueries and use EXISTS or NOT EXISTS for semijoin and antijoin questions.
WITH team_counts AS (
  SELECT team_id, COUNT(*) AS ticket_count
  FROM tickets
  GROUP BY team_id
)
SELECT teams.name, COALESCE(team_counts.ticket_count, 0) AS ticket_count
FROM teams
LEFT JOIN team_counts USING (team_id)
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM tickets
  WHERE tickets.team_id = teams.team_id AND status = 'investigating'
)
ORDER BY teams.name;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.text: NOT IN is always equivalent to NOT EXISTS when NULL appears. This fails the Correlated Exists contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-correlated-exists-4g1aey" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; subquery, CTE, recursion, or set-operation evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-correlated-exists-4g1aey
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
WITH unresolved AS (
  SELECT ticket_id, team_id FROM tickets WHERE status <> 'resolved'
)
SELECT team_id, COUNT(*) AS unresolved_count
FROM unresolved GROUP BY team_id ORDER BY team_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
